title: RSS 2.0, Atom 1.0, Namespaces, and Extension Policy · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent community archive receives a different publisher mix, malformed feed, validator state, tenant, schedule, database condition, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers rss 2.0, atom 1.0, namespaces, and extension policy into a changed standards-aware syndication vocabulary map. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of standards-aware syndication vocabulary map with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through predicti…
objectives: Map RSS channel and item required, optional, repeated, GUID, enclosure, category, source, date, and extension constructs.
Map Atom feed and entry identifiers, authors, links, text constructs, content, categories, dates, xml:base, and xml:lang.
Compare XML expanded names rather than prefixes and preserve unknown extension provenance without inventing semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Rss Channel Item
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 8506 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove RSS and Atom format semantics, expanded names, variance, and extension restraint; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Map RSS channel and item required, optional, repeated, GUID, enclosure, category, source, date, and extension constructs.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Rss Channel Item.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for map rss channel and item required, optional, repeated, guid, enclosure, category, source, date, and extension constructs. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Rss Channel Item, keep Feed Go Rss Channel Item connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. options.text: State the Feed Go Rss Channel Item preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
Every RSS item has a globally unique link and RFC 3339 date. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.

For commit to the mission model, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof.
